title: Implement IP/CoAP transport for Python TUI native client
description: Why this exists: native clients also need the standards-native IP path defined by the Local Client Interface. What needs to be done: implement connection and request/observe flows for IPv6 + CoAP over a local node link, including discovery, status/config resources, messaging resources, mesh state resources, and error handling. Acceptance criteria: client can address a local node over the LCI path; CoAP resource paths and content formats are aligned with spec section 17 and project-LICHEN-py4c; observe/streaming behavior is represented in the shared app model; native_sim or fake-server tests cover success, timeout, malformed response, and unsupported-resource cases.
notes: Implemented Python IP/CoAP ResourceTransport for native LCI clients. Added AiocoapResourceTransport and AiocoapResourceSubscription in python/src/lichen/client/ip_coap.py, with base URI/path composition, GET/POST/PUT/DELETE mapping, CBOR/text/raw payload decoding into CoapResult, content_format and Location-Path preservation, Observe subscription support, owned/injected context lifecycle, timeout/error wrapping, malformed URI wrapping, idempotent close, reconnect ownership handling, and active observe-reader wakeup on close. Added python/tests/client/test_ip_coap.py with fake aiocoap context/handle/observation tests for CBOR response details, outbound CBOR payload/content format, 4.04 preservation, timeout wrapping, invalid CBOR, unsupported method, malformed URI wrapping, Observe initial/later notifications, Observe initial timeout, close while notification read pending, injected-context close, reconnect ownership, and LciClient get_status through in-memory aiocoap transport. Validation on local Python env: cd python && uv run ruff check src/lichen/client tests/client -> PASS; cd python && uv run mypy src/lichen/client tests/client -> PASS (Success: no issues found in 10 source files); cd python && uv run pytest tests/client -q -> PASS (39 passed in 0.07s); git diff --check -> PASS. EC2/Zephyr not used for this Python-only no-hardware slice. Reviews: two independent reviewers found P2 issues in context ownership, Observe initial timeout, message construction wrapping, idempotent cancel, and pending Observe close wakeup; all fixed. Final reviews from Turing and Averroes reported no P0/P1/P2 findings. Residual risk: real UDP/IPv6 link-local behavior and broader section 17 resource-map integration remain outside this no-hardware slice; fake and in-memory aiocoap tests cover transport semantics.
close_reason: Completed Python IP/CoAP LCI ResourceTransport with fake and in-memory aiocoap tests, passing checks, and clean reviews.
